task: I’ve got a small insurance claim and I’m trying not to make it harder than it needs to be. What should I gather before I call, and what usually has to happen after that so I don’t miss any follow-up?

turns.agent_response:
Here’s the simple version.

## Before you call
Gather:
- policy number
- date and time of the loss
- what happened, in plain words
- photos or video
- list of damaged items
- rough value of the damage
- police report number, if there is one
- any receipts, estimates, or repair bills
- contact info for anyone else involved

## On the call
Ask:
- what’s covered
- what your deductible is
- whether they need more photos or forms
- if you need a claim number
- who your adjuster is
- what the next step and timeline are

## After the call
Write down:
- claim number
- adjuster name and phone/email
- date of the call
- anything they asked you to send
- deadlines for paperwork or inspections

## Don’t miss follow-up
- send requested documents right away
- keep copies of everything
- write down every call and message
- don’t throw away damaged items until they say it’s okay
- keep receipts for temporary repairs or expenses

## Call someone / don’t guess if
- you’re not sure what was damaged
- there’s a safety issue
- the loss involves water, fire, theft, or a third party
- you need to know whether to repair or wait

If you want, I can turn this into a **short claim checklist** you can keep by the phone.

user_simulator.text: That’s about what I needed. Plain enough. I’d want the claim number, adjuster’s name, what they need from me, and any deadline written down right away. And I’d keep the receipts and not toss anything until they say so. Simple.
